gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The classified OS errno — never a bare raw code (C3: no opaque error codes)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the RFC-0013 \"errno-class\" column in the diagnostic record. Each variant"] # [doc = " maps a POSIX errno *class* to a typed value; the raw OS code is preserved in the"] # [doc = " `Os` variant of `FsErr` for tooling, but is *never* the primary identifier."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum ErrnoClass { # [doc = " ENOENT / ENOTDIR — the path or a component was absent."] NotFound , # [doc = " EACCES / EPERM — permission denied by the OS."] PermDenied , # [doc = " EEXIST — a path already exists where it should not."] AlreadyExists , # [doc = " ENOTDIR — a path component is not a directory."] NotADirectory , # [doc = " EISDIR — a path is a directory where a regular file was expected."] IsADirectory , # [doc = " ENOTEMPTY — a directory was not empty when remove was attempted."] NotEmpty , # [doc = " ENOSPC / EDQUOT — the disk or quota is full."] DiskFull , # [doc = " EXDEV — a cross-device rename/link was attempted."] CrossDevice , # [doc = " EWOULDBLOCK / EAGAIN — the operation would block."] WouldBlock , # [doc = " EINTR — the operation was interrupted by a signal."] Interrupted , # [doc = " ELOOP — too many levels of symbolic links."] Loop , # [doc = " Some other errno class not covered above; raw code preserved for tooling."] Other , }
gaps.reason: dropped non-doc attribute(s) on enum `ErrnoClass`: # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)]

gaps.snippet: impl fmt :: Display for ErrnoClass { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { match self { Self :: NotFound => write ! (f , "NotFound") , Self :: PermDenied => write ! (f , "PermDenied") , Self :: AlreadyExists => write ! (f , "AlreadyExists") , Self :: NotADirectory => write ! (f , "NotADirectory") , Self :: IsADirectory => write ! (f , "IsADirectory") , Self :: NotEmpty => write ! (f , "NotEmpty") , Self :: DiskFull => write ! (f , "DiskFull") , Self :: CrossDevice => write ! (f , "CrossDevice") , Self :: WouldBlock => write ! (f , "WouldBlock") , Self :: Interrupted => write ! (f , "Interrupted") , Self :: Loop => write ! (f , "Loop") , Self :: Other => write ! (f , "Other") , } } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `fmt` signature: unsupported Rust type form `& mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >`

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The explicit, traceable filesystem error (RFC-0013 diagnostic record)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Every `FsErr` carries:"] # [doc = " - **`path`** — the path that was attempted (for the caller to surface; G11)"] # [doc = " - **`errno_class`** — the classified errno, never a bare code (C3)"] # [doc = " - **`why`** — a human-readable one-liner explaining the refusal (G11)"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " For `UseAfterConsume` the `path` is the handle's original path (if known) and the"] # [doc = " errno_class is absent (the violation is caught above the OS floor — no syscall ran)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 — never-silent"] # [doc = " A caller cannot reach an `FsErr` that is empty, zeroed, or opaque. Every variant"] # [doc = " names the failing operation and the cause. The `Os` variant preserves the raw"] # [doc = " errno for tooling (the `myc-sec` `wild`-audit), but it is never the *primary*"] # [doc = " identifier that a caller pattern-matches on."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum FsErr { # [doc = " The path was not found (and `create`/`create_new` was not set)."] NotFound { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " Permission denied by the OS."] PermDenied { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " The path already exists (with `create_new` set, or `remove`/`rename` collision)."] AlreadyExists { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " A component of the path is not a directory."] NotADirectory { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " The path is a directory where a regular file was expected."] IsADirectory { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " A directory was not empty when `remove_dir` was attempted."] NotEmpty { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " The disk or quota is full."] DiskFull { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " A cross-device rename was attempted."] CrossDevice { from : String , to : String , why : & 'static str , } , # [doc = " The operation would block (non-blocking mode)."] WouldBlock { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " The operation was interrupted by a signal; the caller should retry."] Interrupted { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " Too many levels of symbolic links."] Loop { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " An affine `substrate` handle was used after it was consumed (LR-8)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is caught *above* the OS floor — no syscall ran."] UseAfterConsume { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " The requested `OpenOptions` combination is invalid (e.g. `truncate` without write/append)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " A caller error in the *request*, not a property of the path — caught *above* the OS floor"] # [doc = " (no syscall ran). Distinct from `NotFound`/`PermDenied` so a caller pattern-matching on the"] # [doc = " variant is not misled (C3)."] InvalidOptions { path : String , why : & 'static str } , # [doc = " A lower-level IO failure (threaded, not swallowed — I1)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This wraps a failure that came from the byte-transfer layer. The"] # [doc = " `errno_class` field carries the classification; `raw` is preserved"] # [doc = " for the `myc-sec` `wild`-audit tooling (never the primary discriminant)."] Os { path : String , errno_class : ErrnoClass , # [doc = " Raw OS error code (preserved for tooling; never the primary identifier — C3)."] raw : i32 , why : & 'static str , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `FsErr` variant `NotFound` uses named fields — `constructor ::= Ident ('(' type_ref (',' type_ref)* ')')?` has no named-field/record form

gaps.snippet: impl FsErr { # [doc = " The path that was attempted, if applicable."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns the primary path for single-path errors. For `CrossDevice`, returns the"] # [doc = " `from` path. For `UseAfterConsume`, returns the handle's original path."] # [must_use] pub fn path (& self) -> & str { match self { Self :: NotFound { path , .. } | Self :: PermDenied { path , .. } | Self :: AlreadyExists { path , .. } | Self :: NotADirectory { path , .. } | Self :: IsADirectory { path , .. } | Self :: NotEmpty { path , .. } | Self :: DiskFull { path , .. } | Self :: WouldBlock { path , .. } | Self :: Interrupted { path , .. } | Self :: Loop { path , .. } | Self :: UseAfterConsume { path , .. } | Self :: InvalidOptions { path , .. } | Self :: Os { path , .. } => path , Self :: CrossDevice { from , .. } => from , } } # [doc = " The human-readable why-string (G11 dual projection)."] # [must_use] pub fn why (& self) -> & str { match self { Self :: NotFound { why , .. } | Self :: PermDenied { why , .. } | Self :: AlreadyExists { why , .. } | Self :: NotADirectory { why , .. } | Self :: IsADirectory { why , .. } | Self :: NotEmpty { why , .. } | Self :: DiskFull { why , .. } | Self :: CrossDevice { why , .. } | Self :: WouldBlock { why , .. } | Self :: Interrupted { why , .. } | Self :: Loop { why , .. } | Self :: UseAfterConsume { why , .. } | Self :: InvalidOptions { why , .. } | Self :: Os { why , .. } => why , } } # [doc = " The classified errno — `None` for `UseAfterConsume` (caught above the OS floor)."] # [must_use] pub fn errno_class (& self) -> Option < ErrnoClass > { match self { Self :: NotFound { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: NotFound) , Self :: PermDenied { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: PermDenied) , Self :: AlreadyExists { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: AlreadyExists) , Self :: NotADirectory { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: NotADirectory) , Self :: IsADirectory { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: IsADirectory) , Self :: NotEmpty { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: NotEmpty) , Self :: DiskFull { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: DiskFull) , Self :: CrossDevice { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: CrossDevice) , Self :: WouldBlock { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: WouldBlock) , Self :: Interrupted { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: Interrupted) , Self :: Loop { .. } => Some (ErrnoClass :: Loop) , Self :: UseAfterConsume { .. } => None , Self :: InvalidOptions { .. } => None , Self :: Os { errno_class , .. } => Some (errno_class . clone ()) , } } }

gaps.snippet: impl fmt :: Display for FsErr { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { match self { Self :: CrossDevice { from , to , why } => { write ! (f , "CrossDevice(from={from:?}, to={to:?}): {why}") } Self :: UseAfterConsume { path , why } => { write ! (f , "UseAfterConsume(path={path:?}): {why}") } Self :: InvalidOptions { path , why } => { write ! (f , "InvalidOptions(path={path:?}): {why}") } Self :: Os { path , errno_class , raw , why , } => { write ! (f , "Os(path={path:?}, errno={errno_class}, raw={raw}): {why}") } err => { let class = err . errno_class () . map (| c | c . to_string ()) . unwrap_or_else (| | "Unknown" . to_owned ()) ; write ! (f , "{class}(path={:?}): {}" , err . path () , err . why ()) } } } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " Every FsErr variant exposes a non-empty `why` (G11 — dual projection)."] # [doc = " Guard: an empty `why` in any variant makes this fail."] # [test] fn every_variant_has_a_non_empty_why () { let errors : Vec < FsErr > = vec ! [FsErr :: NotFound { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: PermDenied { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: AlreadyExists { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: NotADirectory { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: IsADirectory { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: NotEmpty { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: DiskFull { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: CrossDevice { from : "/a" . into () , to : "/b" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: WouldBlock { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: Interrupted { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: Loop { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: UseAfterConsume { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: InvalidOptions { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: Os { path : "/a" . into () , errno_class : ErrnoClass :: Other , raw : 42 , why : "test" , } ,] ; for e in & errors { assert ! (! e . why () . is_empty () , "FsErr variant has empty why: {e:?}") ; } } # [doc = " `UseAfterConsume` has no errno class (caught above the OS floor)."] # [doc = " Guard: assigning an errno_class to UseAfterConsume makes this fail."] # [test] fn use_after_consume_has_no_errno_class () { let e = FsErr :: UseAfterConsume { path : "/x" . into () , why : "handle consumed" , } ; assert_eq ! (e . errno_class () , None , "UseAfterConsume must not carry an errno_class (caught above the floor)") ; } # [doc = " Every non-UseAfterConsume variant has a classified errno (C3: no opaque codes)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning None for a non-UseAfterConsume variant makes this fail."] # [test] fn every_os_level_variant_has_errno_class () { let errors = vec ! [FsErr :: NotFound { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: PermDenied { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: AlreadyExists { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } , FsErr :: DiskFull { path : "/a" . into () , why : "test" , } ,] ; for e in & errors { assert ! (e . errno_class () . is_some () , "OS-level error must have an errno_class: {e:?}") ; } } # [doc = " The `Display` for `FsErr` includes the path (so failures are traceable)."] # [doc = " Guard: a Display that drops the path makes this fail."] # [test] fn display_includes_path () { let e = FsErr :: NotFound { path : "/some/path" . into () , why : "absent" , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("/some/path") , "FsErr Display must include the path; got: {s}") ; } }
